results.reason: The literal reverse-scan rule rewrites neighbors in place; identical original 3x3 patches require different outputs. A one-Conv cost-910 implementation is therefore impossible, while truthful unrolling exceeds the incumbent.
Requires connected-component flood fill, component-size extrema, and a second recoloring flood. A static truthful <=30-grid unroll has a structural cost above the incumbent.
The supplied Sakana rule disagrees with the authoritative generator on 35/2000 fresh cases, so it is not a sound compilation target.

results.candidate_known: complete in both local and official verifier
results.reason: Independent fresh accuracy is below the user-authorized 95% floor.
Closed-versus-broken rectangles range to 5x5. Radius-one patches conflict; a dense truthful 5x5 final Conv alone costs 2510, above the incumbent.
A 1x19 row model cannot distinguish rectangle side rows from unrelated endpoints and has conflicting patches. Adding the required vertical context makes the final dense Conv exceed the incumbent.
Selects the globally largest empty rectangle and draws its boundary. Local patches conflict; truthful global integral/selection tensors exceed the incumbent.
Dynamic edge colors are projected by whole-row/column equality before the center is cleared. Radius-one patches conflict and truthful row/column reductions exceed the incumbent.

The rule is exact local 2/3 adjacency, but any ordinary truthful mask is at least 900 bytes and a dense 3x3 final Conv has 910 parameters, both above cost 197. Giant floating Einsum compression is prohibited.
